items.rationale: Sees a step-change in proving performance unlocking practical ZK across the stack; ZK is treated as the central enabling primitive for scaling, privacy, and provenance.

citations.quote: We've developed a new lookup argument called Lasso that is already 10x faster than halo2, with a clear path to 40x.
the result is much faster SNARKs
research has accelerated radically as a result of web3

items.rationale: Believes only networks architected to resist centralization preserve creator monetization and client diversity; Farcaster is his model for a neutral, open social platform.
items.age_note: Core thesis from mid-2022; reaffirmed July 2023 in Nostr/Fediverse critique, so current.

citations.quote: Farcaster is the neutral platform we wanted all along.
Because of its network architecture, Nostr likely degrades to centralization — like Fediverse.

items.rationale: Scaling is a named prerequisite for adoption; actively ships L2 infrastructure (Magi, Helios, Magi) and pushes data-availability sampling to cut storage costs.

citations.quote: Magi is a new OP Stack client for the Optimism ecosystem... client diversity = robust decentralization
we unpack danksharding and data availability sampling, which would radically decrease the cost of storing data on Ethereum
I personally think scaling, privacy, and something relating to key management/auth/identity are critical prerequisites.

items.rationale: Treats privacy as a missing, near-term-arriving requirement for mainstream crypto, including compliance-provable privacy designs.

citations.quote: And privacy is coming to crypto.
Cicada is the cleanest app layer private voting I've seen
Realistically, we can't have mainstream privacy without it.

items.rationale: Frames crypto as giving AIs the ability to own and transact; excited by autonomous, self-funding programs and 1-person / 0-person companies.

citations.quote: AI = how machines can think; crypto = how machines can own; AI + crypto = how machines can think on their own
the first 0-person unicorn? An autonomous program on a crypto network that can fund and grow itself.

items.rationale: Wants a permissionless rights layer so derivative creative works can be composed and monetized; sees networked, recursive creativity as a native crypto use case.
items.age_note: Newest theme (Sep 2023), stated with high enthusiasm.

citations.quote: Story Protocol fixes this with an onchain remix graph that makes composition and attribution natural.
preserving monetization everyone along in authorship tree.

items.rationale: Argues a single SNARK-verification opcode would make bitcoin fully programmable and revive Satoshi's broader vision; wants ZK 'brought home' to bitcoin.
items.age_note: Very recent (Sep 2023); a newly emphasized direction.

citations.quote: adding a VERIFY_SNARK opcode to the bitcoin protocol — would make bitcoin totally programmable and free to move all over the internet.
We should bring zero knowledge back home to bitcoin.

items.rationale: Pushes formal methods and symbolic testing as the path to making crypto safer; ships open-source tooling toward this end.

citations.quote: We hope Halmos will be the best symbolic testing tool in Ethereum... let's make crypto safer.
With formal methods supporting other kinds of tests, we can make Ethereum safer.

items.rationale: Sees smart accounts (identity + ownership, not just wallets) as enabling counter-intuitive new behaviors and better key management.

citations.quote: Account abstraction enables smart accounts. What are some counter-intuitive possibilities enabled by smart accounts?

items.rationale: Strongly holds that the goal is a well-designed protocol; tokens are a tool (~5% of the work) and pie-chart 'tokenomics' consulting is worthless.

citations.quote: A protocol needs a clear goal. Launching a token isn't a goal.
A made up pie chart is not real work.

items.rationale: Long-held belief that on-chain games will fill the programmer-game niche and reach Dwarf Fortress-level depth; enjoys collaborating with the games team.
items.age_note: First stated 2022-06, restated 2023-04, so persisted and current.

citations.quote: Every game can be as rich and deep as Dwarf Fortress.
I believe fully on-chain games will fill a similar niche in coming years.

items.rationale: Wants login unbundled and keys reused across contexts; views biometric and social-graph personhood approaches as complementary parts of decentralized identity.
items.age_note: Heaviest in 2022 (Spruce/SIWE); lightly reaffirmed July 2023 via personhood discussion, so still held.

citations.quote: Spruce is building the full stack of open source tools and interop standards needed to unbundle the login
Any robust decentralized concept of personhood will incorporate all of the above.

items.rationale: His most repeated grievance: closed platforms cripple, block, or tax native crypto/mobile experiences and suppress innovation; the open browser is the antidote.

citations.quote: Apple will cripple you, block you, or take your lunch if you try to offer a native experience.
The effects of Apple's 30% fee on innovation are radically underestimated

items.rationale: Warns that networks not architected to resist centralization (relays, federation without real exit rights) functionally re-centralize.

citations.quote: Because of its network architecture, Nostr likely degrades to centralization — like Fediverse. This happens often in simple p2p systems.
Federation doesn't necessarily include exit rights... Functionally centralized.

items.rationale: Sharply attacked Binance's PoR as unverifiable and exploitable (no Merkle tree, negative-balance flaw); distrusts centralized exchange attestations.
items.age_note: From the Nov-2022 FTX/Binance aftermath; not restated in 2023, but a clearly held stance during that window.

citations.quote: Binance's system is useless and easily exploited by them.
Negative balances are a known fatal flaw of the 'proof of reserves' method Binance shared

items.rationale: Notes a structural hazard: since only the proof is shared, malicious inputs to ZK circuits can't be scrutinized, making exploits hard to diagnose.

citations.quote: Security bugs in ZK circuits will be challenging. Since only the proof is shared, the malicious input can't be scrutinized

items.rationale: Sees AI-detecting-AI as a losing approach and is recruiting crypto provenance/attestation techniques as the more durable defense.

citations.quote: AI-based techniques for identifying AI seem unlikely to succeed.
crypto vs. deepfakes — who has been thinking about ways crypto techniques can be used to resist fake content?

items.rationale: Frustrated by closed/regressing LLMs and gated weight releases; views open source as the only way to preserve capability and democratize knowledge.

citations.quote: It's nearly useless now. Open source is the only way to preserve the flame.
why not share the LLaMA weights publicly? Sharing only with governments and academics... was nonsense.

items.rationale: Believes bitcoin's security/economic model must change to survive coming decades (subsidy decline), separate from his excitement about its programmability.

citations.quote: the security model of bitcoin needs to change if it's going to last the next few decades.

items.rationale: Dismisses headline metrics that ignore user/developer adoption and revenue as 'very bad,' and repeatedly flags inaccurate/sensational crypto press.

citations.quote: It also doesn't reflect user or developer adoption, and doesn't directly map onto revenue for most protocols. It's a very bad metric.

items.rationale: His platform of deepest commitment — ships clients and research for it, calls it the clear leader with overwhelming developer mindshare and a long scaling roadmap. Framed as ecosystem/tech conviction, not a price call.
items.age_note: Leadership claim from May 2022 State of Crypto, but continuously reinforced through 2023 build work.

citations.quote: other layer 1 blockchains have grown rapidly... but Ethereum is the clear leader. Its overwhelming mindshare among web3 devs
The future is onchain.

items.rationale: Excited about its latent potential IF made programmable via a SNARK opcode and ZK; this excitement is explicitly conditional and paired with concern about its security model.

citations.quote: All of the things Satoshi envisioned become possible again.
We should bring zero knowledge back home to bitcoin.

risk_regime.rationale: Lazzarin is a structurally bullish long-term builder, not a market timer. He frames crypto as ~1995-early ('we're still so early'), keeps building through 'crypto winter,' and prizes 'crypto-the-computer' over 'crypto-the-casino.' His optimism is about technology trajectory and adoption prerequisites (scaling, privacy, identity), not about price or near-term positioning — so 'risk_on' here means conviction in the technology's durability rather than a tactical market bet.

notes: Lazzarin is a16z crypto's engineering/CTO voice; his corpus is almost entirely technical/architectural, so 'sectors' map to primitives and infrastructure (ZK, L2 scaling, privacy, social, identity, security tooling) rather than trading themes. He expresses essentially no token-price views and even argues tokens are merely a protocol-design tool, so tokens_excited is framed as platform/tech conviction and tokens_concerned is empty. Recurring cross-cutting beliefs: (1) 'network design has economic consequences' and architecture must resist centralization; (2) open source preserves capability and accessibility; (3) ZK is the master primitive (scaling, privacy, provenance, even bitcoin programmability). Most recent and intensifying threads (Sep 2023): onchain IP/Story Protocol, bitcoin+ZK programmability, and the 'data publishing' (vs 'data availability') terminology shift. He referenced MKR/AAVE/COMP only conceptually (as protocol risk-underwriting tokens, 2022-07-14), not as investment picks. The Binance proof-of-reserves concern is anchored to the Nov-2022 FTX/Binance window and not restated in 2023.
